satire_attempt: Local engineer claims he is a 'tattva-darśin' after discovering his p99 alert was firing on a dashboard typo. He has assembled a deck titled 'asat-driven engineering' and is presenting Wednesday at brown-bag. Industry insiders predict a 2027 LinkedIn post: 'How discovering my Datadog query was wrong unlocked Brahman-level observability.'
verdict_reason: Mocking the verse requires inventing the claim that finding a metric-pipeline bug makes someone enlightened. The engineering layer makes no such claim — it explicitly says the seer of truth has 'done the work — replayed the queries, traced the pipeline' and is not a posture but the result of investigation. The falsifiability rules out the move my satire would land on (declaring intuited bugs as asat). My satire only works against a strawman the verse actively disclaims. PASS.
